response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development, including C and C++ roles (National Instruments), but lacks direct experience with modern C++ (C++11/14/17/20) and STL for high-performance industrial automation. Their expertise is heavily skewed toward web, mobile, and scripting languages (Node.js, TypeScript, DLang) rather than C++-centric systems programming or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443). While their problem-solving and architectural skills are strong, the alignment with core technical competencies for this role is weak.
strengths: Strong background in C and C++ (National Instruments, low-latency apps with DLang)
Proven problem-solving and architectural design skills (event-driven platforms, Kubernetes, microservices)
Experience with test-driven development (TDD) and secure coding practices
concerns: Limited recent C++ experience (last C++ role was in 2015; current work in TypeScript, Node.js, DLang)
No demonstrated exposure to industrial automation protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, OPC UA) or cloud platforms (Azure)
No evidence of applying IEC 62443 standards or secure development lifecycle (SDL) in C++ contexts
